_coverage: partial: [--help, --version, --list-input-formats, --list-output-formats, --list-extensions, --list-highlight-languages, --list-highlight-styles, --bash-completion, --print-default-template, --print-default-data-file, --print-highlight-style, --dump-citeproc skipped (meta/info flags that print and exit); --variable-json omitted (complex JSON input not suitable for GUI); --html-q-tags, --link-images, --epub-subdirectory, --chunk-template, --progress-template omitted (very niche)]
description: Universal document converter. Converts between markup formats including Markdown, HTML, LaTeX, Word docx, EPUB, PDF, and many more.

arguments.name: Input Format
arguments.description: Input format. Extensions can be enabled/disabled with +EXT/-EXT syntax. Use --list-input-formats for full list.

arguments.name: Output Format
arguments.description: Output format. Extensions can be enabled/disabled with +EXT/-EXT syntax. Use --list-output-formats for full list.

arguments.name: Output File
arguments.description: Write output to FILE instead of stdout. Required for binary formats (docx, odt, epub, pdf). Use '-' for stdout.

arguments.name: Data Directory
arguments.description: User data directory for pandoc data files (templates, reference docs, etc). Overrides default location.

arguments.name: Defaults File
arguments.description: YAML or JSON file with default option settings. Searched in working directory, then defaults/ subdirectory of user data directory.

arguments.name: Verbose
arguments.description: Give verbose debugging output.

arguments.name: Quiet
arguments.description: Suppress warning messages.

arguments.name: Fail If Warnings
arguments.description: Exit with error status if there are any warnings.

arguments.name: Log File
arguments.description: Write log messages in machine-readable JSON format to FILE.

arguments.name: Shift Heading Level By
arguments.description: Shift heading levels by a positive or negative integer. Use -1 when converting docs that use H1 for title.

arguments.name: Base Header Level
arguments.description: Specify the base level for headings (defaults to 1).

arguments.name: Indented Code Classes
arguments.description: Classes to use for indented code blocks. Multiple classes separated by spaces or commas.

arguments.name: Default Image Extension
arguments.description: Default extension for images with no extension. Affects Markdown and LaTeX readers.

arguments.name: File Scope
arguments.description: Parse each file individually before combining. Allows footnotes in different files with same identifiers to work.

arguments.name: Filter
arguments.description: External filter program (reads JSON from stdin, writes JSON to stdout). Applied in command-line order.

arguments.name: Lua Filter
arguments.description: Lua filter script for transforming the document. Applied in command-line order with --filter and --citeproc.

arguments.name: Metadata
arguments.description: Set metadata field KEY to VAL. Format: KEY=VAL or KEY:VAL. If no value, treated as boolean true.

arguments.name: Metadata File
arguments.description: Read metadata from YAML or JSON file. Can be used repeatedly; later files take precedence.

arguments.name: Preserve Tabs
arguments.description: Preserve tabs instead of converting them to spaces. Only affects tabs in literal code spans and code blocks.

arguments.name: Tab Stop
arguments.description: Number of spaces per tab.

arguments.name: Track Changes
arguments.description: How to handle MS Word Track Changes. Only affects the docx reader.

arguments.name: Extract Media
arguments.description: Extract images and media from source document to this directory and adjust references.

arguments.name: Abbreviations
arguments.description: Custom abbreviations file, one per line. Used by the Markdown reader for non-breaking spaces.

arguments.name: Standalone
arguments.description: Produce output with appropriate header and footer (e.g. a valid HTML file, not a fragment). Auto-set for pdf, epub, docx, odt.

arguments.name: Template
arguments.description: Custom template file for the generated document. Implies --standalone.

arguments.name: Variable
arguments.description: Set template variable KEY=VAL. Can be used multiple times. Values accumulate into lists if key is repeated.

arguments.name: Sandbox
arguments.description: Run in sandbox mode, limiting IO operations. Recommended for untrusted input.

arguments.name: EOL
arguments.description: Line ending style for output.

arguments.name: DPI
arguments.description: Default DPI for pixel-to-inch/cm conversion.

arguments.name: Wrap
arguments.description: Text wrapping in output source code. 'auto' wraps to --columns width, 'none' disables wrapping, 'preserve' keeps source wrapping.

arguments.name: Columns
arguments.description: Line length in characters for text wrapping and plain text table column widths.

arguments.name: Table of Contents
arguments.description: Include an automatically generated table of contents. Requires --standalone. No effect on man, docbook, jats output.

arguments.name: TOC Depth
arguments.description: Number of section levels to include in the table of contents.

arguments.name: List of Figures
arguments.description: Include a list of figures. Only affects latex, context, and docx output. Requires --standalone.

arguments.name: List of Tables
arguments.description: Include a list of tables. Only affects latex, context, and docx output. Requires --standalone.

arguments.name: Strip Comments
arguments.description: Strip out HTML comments in Markdown or Textile source rather than passing them through.

arguments.name: Syntax Highlighting
arguments.description: Method for code syntax highlighting. Use a style name, 'none', 'default', 'idiomatic', or a .theme file path.

arguments.name: No Highlight
arguments.description: Disables syntax highlighting for code blocks and inlines.

arguments.name: Highlight Style
arguments.description: Coloring style for highlighted source code.

arguments.name: Syntax Definition
arguments.description: KDE XML syntax definition file for additional language highlighting support.

arguments.name: Include In Header
arguments.description: Include contents of FILE at end of header. Implies --standalone. Can be repeated.

arguments.name: Include Before Body
arguments.description: Include contents of FILE at beginning of document body. Implies --standalone. Can be repeated.

arguments.name: Include After Body
arguments.description: Include contents of FILE at end of document body. Implies --standalone. Can be repeated.

arguments.name: Resource Path
arguments.description: Colon-separated (Unix) or semicolon-separated (Windows) list of paths to search for images and resources.

arguments.name: Request Header
arguments.description: Set HTTP request header NAME:VAL when fetching resources from URLs.

arguments.name: No Check Certificate
arguments.description: Disable HTTPS certificate verification. Use for self-signed or expired certificates.

arguments.name: Embed Resources
arguments.description: Produce standalone HTML with all resources embedded as data: URIs. Works only with HTML output formats.

arguments.name: Self Contained
arguments.description: Produce standalone HTML with embedded resources.

arguments.name: ASCII
arguments.description: Use only ASCII characters in output. Supported for XML, HTML, Markdown, roff, and limited LaTeX.

arguments.name: Section Divs
arguments.description: Wrap sections in <section> tags (or <div> for html4) and attach identifiers to the enclosing tag.

arguments.name: Email Obfuscation
arguments.description: Method for obfuscating mailto: links in HTML.

arguments.name: ID Prefix
arguments.description: Prefix for all auto-generated identifiers in HTML/DocBook output and footnote numbers in Markdown.

arguments.name: Title Prefix
arguments.description: Prefix for the HTML header title. Implies --standalone.

arguments.name: CSS
arguments.description: Link to a CSS style sheet. Can be repeated for multiple files. Only affects HTML and EPUB output.

arguments.name: Reference Links
arguments.description: Use reference-style links instead of inline links in Markdown/reStructuredText output.

arguments.name: Reference Location
arguments.description: Where to place footnotes and reference links.

arguments.name: Markdown Headings
arguments.description: Heading style for Markdown output.

arguments.name: Figure Caption Position
arguments.description: Whether figure captions go above or below figures.

arguments.name: Table Caption Position
arguments.description: Whether table captions go above or below tables.

arguments.name: Top Level Division
arguments.description: Treat top-level headings as this division type in LaTeX, ConTeXt, DocBook, TEI output.

arguments.name: Number Sections
arguments.description: Number section headings in LaTeX, ConTeXt, HTML, Docx, ms, or EPUB output.

arguments.name: Number Offset
arguments.description: Offsets for section heading numbers, comma-separated. Implies --number-sections.

arguments.name: Incremental
arguments.description: Make list items in slide shows display incrementally (one by one).

arguments.name: Slide Level
arguments.description: Heading level that creates slides. Headings above become section dividers, below become subheads.

arguments.name: Reference Doc
arguments.description: Style reference file for docx, ODT, or PowerPoint output. Content is ignored; styles/properties are used.

arguments.name: List Tables
arguments.description: Render tables as list tables in RST output.

arguments.name: EPUB Cover Image
arguments.description: Image file to use as EPUB cover. Recommended: less than 1000px width/height.

arguments.name: EPUB Title Page
arguments.description: Whether to include a title page in the EPUB.

arguments.name: EPUB Metadata
arguments.description: XML file containing Dublin Core metadata elements for the EPUB.

arguments.name: EPUB Embed Font
arguments.description: Font file to embed in the EPUB. Can be repeated. Supports wildcards.

arguments.name: Split Level
arguments.description: Heading level at which to split EPUB or chunked HTML into separate files.

arguments.name: EPUB Chapter Level
arguments.description: Heading level for EPUB chapter splitting.

arguments.name: PDF Engine
arguments.description: Engine for PDF generation. Default depends on output format.

arguments.name: PDF Engine Option
arguments.description: Command-line argument to pass to the PDF engine. Can be repeated.

arguments.name: Citeproc
arguments.description: Process citations, replacing them with rendered citations and adding a bibliography. Requires --bibliography or bibliography metadata.

arguments.name: Bibliography
arguments.description: Bibliography file for citation processing. Can be repeated for multiple files. Supports BibTeX, BibLaTeX, CSL JSON/YAML, and more.

arguments.name: CSL
arguments.description: CSL stylesheet for citation formatting. Default: chicago-author-date.

arguments.name: Citation Abbreviations
arguments.description: File with citation abbreviations in CSL JSON format.

arguments.name: Natbib
arguments.description: Use natbib for citations in LaTeX output. Not for use with --citeproc or PDF output. For producing .tex files processed with bibtex.

arguments.name: Biblatex
arguments.description: Use biblatex for citations in LaTeX output. Not for use with --citeproc or PDF output. For producing .tex files processed with biber.

arguments.name: MathJax
arguments.description: Use MathJax to render TeX math in HTML. Optional URL to MathJax script; if omitted, Cloudflare CDN is used.

arguments.name: MathML
arguments.description: Convert TeX math to MathML. Used with HTML and Docbook output formats.

arguments.name: WebTeX
arguments.description: Convert TeX formulas to tags linking to an external rendering service.

arguments.name: KaTeX
arguments.description: Use KaTeX to render TeX math in HTML. Optional URL to KaTeX files.

arguments.name: GladTeX
arguments.description: Enclose TeX math in <eq> tags for gladTeX processing.

arguments.name: IPyNB Output
arguments.description: How to treat ipynb output cells.

arguments.name: Input Files
arguments.description: Input file(s) to convert. If not specified, reads from stdin. Multiple files are concatenated with blank lines between them.
